title: Mutual Consent Divorce: Timeline, Documents, and Common Mistakes
author: Adv. Ayesha Siddiqui
content: Mutual consent divorce is often the most practical option when both spouses want a clean separation. Even then, it needs careful documentation: marriage proof, address and identity proofs, and clear settlement terms.

The settlement is the heart of the process. It should cover maintenance, return of articles, custody/visitation (if children are involved), and withdrawal of related complaints or cases (only where legally appropriate). Ambiguity here creates delays later.
A common mistake is signing an informal settlement without clarity on payments and deadlines. Put instalments, dates, mode of payment, and consequences of default in writing.
If there are property or loan issues, ensure documents match the settlement. Courts rely on clear terms and consistent documents.
Good preparation reduces hearings and stress. A structured checklist is the fastest route to closure.
